source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In a tournament each of the 6 teams plays one match against every other team. In each round of matches, 3 take place simultaneously. A TV station has already decided which match it will broadcast for each round, as shown in the diagram. In which round will team D play against team F?
<image1>

image_dependency: question explicitly says the TV station choices are 'as shown in the diagram'
the target asks for a specific round based on match scheduling information that is not fully present in text alone
asset registry shows a valid primary image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving likely requires combining round-robin tournament constraints for 6 teams with the broadcast information from the diagram
the question asks for an unshown match round, so the solver must infer schedule structure rather than read off a direct label
the problem involves simultaneous matches per round, adding scheduling constraints across rounds
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value '1'
the tournament structure is well-defined: 6 teams, every pair plays once, 3 matches per round
a proposed solution can be checked against the diagram and round-robin constraints for consistency
asset registry indicates question and answer are present and passed validation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scoring is strong: high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning value, and high verifiability
although the diagram is required and image readability is noted as not perfect, the needed core semantic assets are still available
the numeric answer format supports straightforward verification
